hero.tagline: A ladder-frame seven-seat SUV known for road presence, durability and 4x4 capability.

pricing.notes: Variant prices are ex-showroom reference prices from recent Indian listings; verify against Toyota dealer/city price before publishing final on-road values.

overview.minides: Road presence that speaks before you do — the Fortuner blends rugged capability, premium comfort, and that unmistakable “raasta khud ban jaata hai” aura.
overview.description: The Toyota Fortuner range combines a 2.7L petrol automatic, 2.8L diesel manual and automatic choices, 2WD and 4WD drivetrains, and premium Legender and GR-S trims. It is positioned for buyers who want a durable seven-seat SUV with strong resale value, commanding road presence and long-distance ability.

blog.title: Toyota Fortuner review: the durable seven-seat SUV benchmark
blog.subtitle: Diesel torque, ladder-frame toughness and premium variants keep the Fortuner in a class of its own.
blog.summary: The Fortuner remains one of India's most recognisable premium SUVs. It is expensive, but buyers continue to value its tough construction, Toyota reliability, road presence, diesel 4x4 ability and strong resale demand.

sections.heading: Design and road presence
body: The Fortuner's tall stance, broad grille, large wheels and high bonnet give it the commanding look buyers expect from a full-size SUV. Legender variants add a sharper split-headlamp design and dual-tone detailing, while GR-S brings sportier cosmetic changes.

sections.heading: Engines and variants
body: The range includes a 2.7L petrol automatic for buyers who want a simpler 2WD setup and a 2.8L diesel for the core Fortuner audience. Diesel buyers can choose manual or automatic gearboxes, 2WD or 4WD drivetrains, and premium Legender or GR-S variants.
This file lists 9 India variants across 4 trim groups: Fortuner Petrol, Fortuner Diesel, Legender and GR-S.

sections.heading: Interior, comfort and features
body: The cabin focuses on durability and long-distance comfort. Key features include leather upholstery, ventilated front seats, powered front seats, dual-zone automatic climate control, an 8-inch touchscreen, connected tech, power back door, parking sensors and seven airbags.
Legender and GR-S variants add richer styling touches, wireless charging, ambient lighting, premium exterior detailing and JBL audio on selected 4WD variants.

sections.heading: Safety and off-road ability
body: Toyota equips the Fortuner with 7 airbags, ABS with EBD, brake assist, vehicle stability control, hill assist, traction control, ISOFIX and an anti-theft alarm. The 4WD variants add high and low range, electronic drive control, downhill assist control and a locking differential for tougher terrain.

pros: Excellent road presence and strong resale value
Seven airbags and core safety kit are standard
cons: Very expensive for the feature set
No panoramic sunroof or ADAS in current India brochure
Third row is best for shorter journeys
Petrol range is limited to 2WD automatic in this 9-variant file
blog.verdict: The Toyota Fortuner is best for buyers who value durability, resale, road presence and long-distance confidence over flashy equipment. The diesel 4x2 AT is the easy urban-tourer pick, while diesel 4x4 and GR-S variants are for buyers who want the fullest Fortuner experience.

faqs.question: How many Fortuner variants are in this JSON?
faqs.answer: This file contains 9 variants across 4 trim groups.

faqs.question: Does the Toyota Fortuner have 4WD?
faqs.answer: Yes. Selected diesel, Legender and GR-S variants include 4WD with high and low range hardware.

faqs.question: Does the Toyota Fortuner have ADAS?
faqs.answer: No. ADAS features are marked false because Toyota India's current Fortuner brochure does not list ADAS for this model.

metadata.notes: Structure mirrors punch.json. Current India references show 9 sellable Fortuner/Fortuner Legender/GR-S variants; prices should be rechecked by city/dealer before final publishing.
